_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: Conference Tagline: 'It Just Means More'; Three storylines define the 2026 SEC: (1) the SEC hasn't played for a national title since the 2022 season and the Big Ten has caught it at the top; (2) the SEC remains the deepest conference in football; (3) the move to a nine-game conference schedule is itself a top story — does the extra in-league game hurt CFP résumés (more losses) or help them (tougher games played)? Conference-record calibration for 2026 (apply these bands when framing projected or actual records — they override generic win-total framing): 9-0 in conf would be historic; 8-1 is the realistic ceiling and makes the SEC title game; 7-2 also makes the title game; 6-3 contends; 5-4 is a good SEC season for most teams. A 'tremendous season' here is 8-1 in conf, not nine overall wins. SEC tier framework (used in team_notes; tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record — record varies with schedule strength): Tier 1 = SEC title-game favorite if healthy and CFP-bound; Tier 2 = title-game contender, CFP contender; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game outsider, in the contention mix but outside CFP; Tier 4 = rebuilding year, fighting to get to a bowl game; Tier 5 = down year, where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be the ceiling. When team_notes give a range (e.g., 'Tier 2-3'), the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside and pick a centering tier in the writeup based on health and early-season tape. SEC Championship game is played in Atlanta, Georgia.

team_notes.alabama: QB1 Keelon Russell vs Austin Mack unresolved — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup; HC DeBoer's new contract helps ease tensions; Offense, particularly run game must improve, including both the Offensive Line and running backs; WR unit one of CFB best with Ryan Coleman-Williams, Lotzeir Brooks and portal addition Noah Rogers from NC State; Staff feels this defense is physically bigger and looks like an Alabama defense should; Secondary one of the best in CFB; Revenge game vs Florida State in week 3; Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.arkansas: New HC Silverfield, hired from G6 school Memphis, hasn't won over fans yet, QB1 KJ Jackson vs AJ Hill key storyline — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup; Defense has been at the bottom of the SEC rankings for a few years now; Added 43 players and a total of 179 career starts via transfer portal to rebuild roster; A bowl bid would be a great season for Arkansas. Tier 4-5 SEC team.
team_notes.auburn: Fans starving for new HC Alex Golesh to fix the offense and program; DC Durkin back is key but strange. QB Byrum Brown from South Florida (along with 12 others with Golesh & staff) key storyline. RB and WR should be really good but OL will be built mostly from 8 portal additions. Defense should be solid, defensive coordinator DJ Durkin was retained from previous staff. Likely not a contender, but could surprise some teams. Have not won on the road at Alabama since 2010, and are on a current 0-6 streak overall.  Tier 3-4 SEC team.
team_notes.florida: New HC Jon Sumrall popular but needs to fix the offense, QB1 Aaron Philo vs Tramell Jones Jr. key storyline — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup. New OC Buster Faulkner a great hire from Georgia Tech, brought QB Aaron Philo with him. Have real weapons with RB Jadan Baugh and at WR with Vernell Brown III and Dallas Wilson who flashed as freshman but must remain healthy. Defensive line is good enough to be dominant.  Added 23 players via transfer portal, 10 of which were starters are their previous schools.  Likely not a contender, but could surprise some teams. Have beaten rival Florida State two years in a row. Tier 2-4 SEC team.
team_notes.georgia: SEC back-to-back champs, but 0-2 in CFP quarterfinals in the last two years is disappointing (have had a bye in round 1 both years). QB Gunner Stockton in his second year starting and is a great leader.  RB Nate Frazier a good one, Chauncey Bowens and portal addition Dante Dowdell from Kentucky join to form one of SECs best units.   WR is a big question but Tight End room is loaded with size and talent. OL has 4 starters back but must improve consistency. Defense must also improve consistency. Tier 1 SEC team, one of the favorites to win the SEC this year but not a lock. HC Kirby Smart won back to back titles in 2021 and 2022, NIL era has been different though.
team_notes.kentucky: Renewed hope with new HC Stein, especially on offense after the Stoops era went stale. Excited for QB Kenny Minchey from Notre Dame who adds a mobile threat, RBs are mostly portals like CJ Baxter from University of Texas that hasn't been the same since a knee injury in August of 2024.  TE Willie Rodriguez was good last year and has had a big spring, a real weapon for the offense. OL added 4 starters via transfer portal. DTs are strong but Defense has pass rush questions and inexperienced CBs. Tier 4-5 SEC team.
team_notes.lsu: Kiffin a proven Offensive mastermind, returns to play vs Ole Miss in Week 3. Blake Baker retained as DC, big for defense; QB Sam Leavitt recovery from foot injury last October (while at Arizona State) key. WR unit of Tre' Brown III, Jackson Harris, Jayce Brown and others one of SECs best.  Tight End Trey'Dez Green may be the SECs best weapon most people aren't familiar with yet.  OL built mostly from 10 portal adds including OT Jordan Seaton, a 4 million dollar aquisition from Colorado.  DC Blake Baker retained from previous staff, a good one. Defense should be good.  Kiffin returns to Oxford in week 3 when LSU plays at Ole Miss, a highly anticipated game. Tier 1-3 SEC team.
team_notes.mississippi-state: Prev HC Zach Arnett back as DC; QB Kamario Taylor SO key to fans hope of escaping the SEC cellar. RB Fluff Bothwell a good one but coming off injury last year.  OL has only 1 returning starter and lots of questions although reportedly had a productive spring. Defense looks bottom of SEC caliber on paper, DBs their best unit but front seven just doesn't measure up. Last Egg Bowl win vs arch rival Ole Miss was 2022, on a 1-5 series stretch. Tier 4-5 SEC team.
team_notes.missouri: HC Eliah Drinkwitz one of SECs most underrated coaches, has a 46-29 record at Missouri.  QB Austin Simmons (transferred IN from Ole Miss — new arrival, NOT returning) is the big storyline; defense is a big rebuild job under popular DC Corey Batoon. New OC Chip Lindsey has a lot to prove as his offenses at Michigan in 2025 and North Carolina in 2023-2024 were not good. RB duo of Ahmad Hardy and Jamal Roberts a top SEC unit; OL looks really good with two PFF top rated players lead by OT Cayden Green; added portal WRs headlined by Cayden Lee from Ole Miss.  Defense replacing a lot this year, added 16 defensive players from the portal to help. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.oklahoma: Offense has been the big problem, run game especially. Season will be determined by whether the offensive line has been fixed, as it has been a constant issue for years now. QB1 Mateer wasn't the same last year after a thumb injury, from which he has recovered. Added WRs Trell Harris and Parker Livingston via transfer portal to pair with electric Isaiah Sategna to finally give OU more weapons. Defense has been dominant and should be strength again this year; OU may have the best secondary in CFB lead by CB duo Courtland Guillory and Eli Bowen. Lost two straight to arch rival Texas in Red River Rivalry since joining the SEC. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.ole-miss: Coming off best season maybe ever; want HC Golding to prove he's better than Lane Kiffin (left for LSU last year). Ole Miss get a chance at revenge on Kiffin when they host LSU in week 3, one of CFBs most anticipated matchups. QB Trinidad Chambliss won his case for another year of eligibility, a big boost to the offense and team after his magical performance in the CFP in January.  RB Kewan Lacy may be best in SEC; added 5 WR via transfer portal to replace losses. Defensive line should be elite with DE Suntarine Perkins and DT Will Echoles. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.south-carolina: HC Beamer underappreciated but last year was bad. Hired OC Kendal Briles to fix the offense.  QB LaNorris Sellers a huge talent coming off a disappointing 2025 season after a breakout season in 2024.  RB is a question, Rebuil offensive line with 7 starters added via the transfer portal but spring injuries already a problem at OT, hampers trying to fix this offense. DE Dylan Stewart is a top CFB Edge rusher.  Tier 3-4 SEC team.
team_notes.tennessee: QBs George MacIntyre and 5★ Faizon Brandon are competing for the job — treat as unsettled across all sections of the writeup. RB DeSean Bishop one of the best in the SEC and CFB. WR duo of Mike Matthews and Braylon Staley a great one, and the OL has all five starters back. New DC Jim Knowles hired away from Penn State to fix a poor Tenn defense last year, although they've added good talent.  Portal DE Chaz Coleman missed spring for mental health and may not play this year. Did not beat team w winning record LY. Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.texas: Defining year for HC Sark and the program; they've been building to make a deep CFP run in '26 with QB Arch Manning & portal signings. OL the big question on team. RBs Raleek Brown and Hollywood Smothers are big upgrades, and WR addition Cam Coleman from Auburn was a top rated portal player.  Defense has a great front 7 led by All SEC DE Colin Simmons and portal LB Rasheed Biles from University of Pittsburgh.  Hired Will Muschamp as new DC, a former HC at Florida and South Carolina.  Texas is 0-3 vs Georgia since joining the SEC. Tier 1-2 SEC team, one of the favorites to win the conference and the national title.
team_notes.texas-am: Last year's 0-2 ending and 10-0 CFP loss to Miami plus another loss to rival Texas was hard on fans, but HC Elko is popular even if this year is just decent / transitional. Will have new OC and DC this year, both internal hires. LB and team captain Sanford injured in spring will miss season. QB Marcel Reed was good last year but played poorly in CFP loss to Miami throwing 2 costly INTs.  RB Rueben Owens II a good one, WR Isaiah Horton added via portal from Alabama to join an already electric unit lead by Mario Craver. OL rebuilt with four starters from the transfer portal.  Defense will need portal additions to help fill losses from last year.  Have yet to beat Texas (0-2) since joining the SEC.  Tier 2-3 SEC team.
team_notes.vanderbilt: All-everything QB Diego Pavia graduated after leading Vanderbilt to its best two seasons maybe ever — can they win without him? 5 star freshman QB Jared Curtis may start this fall; RBs Sedrick Alexander and Makhilyn Young form good duo, WR Junior Sherrill joined by Old Dominion transfer Ja'Cory Thomas.  OL is a big question with only one starter back, defense wasn't great last year and must replace a lot of players. Tier 3-5 SEC team.
